name: L-Phenylalanine
description: An essential aromatic amino acid that is a precursor of melanin; dopamine; noradrenalin (norepinephrine), and thyroxine.
indication: L-phenylalanine may be helpful in some with depression. It may also be useful in the treatment of vitiligo. There is some evidence that L-phenylalanine may exacerbate tardive dyskinesia in some schizophrenic patients and in some who have used neuroleptic drugs.
toxicity: L-phenylalanine will exacerbate symptoms of phenylketonuria if used by phenylketonurics. L-phenylalanine was reported to exacerbate tardive dyskinesia when used by some with schizophrenia.
mechanismOfAction:
The mechanism of L-phenylalanine's putative antidepressant activity may be accounted for by its precursor role in the synthesis of the neurotransmitters norepinephrine and dopamine. Elevated brain norepinephrine and dopamine levels are thought to be associated with antidepressant effects.
The mechanism of L-phenylalanine's possible antivitiligo activity is not well understood. It is thought that L-phenylalanine may stimulate the production of melanin in the affected skin
pharmacodynamics: Used by the brain to produce Norepinephrine, a chemical that transmits signals between nerve cells and the brain; keeps you awake and alert; reduces hunger pains; functions as an antidepressant and helps improve memory.

interactions.name: Metamizole
interactions.description: The risk or severity of myelosuppression can be increased when Metamizole is combined with L-Phenylalanine.

interactions.name: Natalizumab
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when L-Phenylalanine is combined with Natalizumab.

interactions.name: Pimecrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Pimecrolimus is combined with L-Phenylalanine.

interactions.name: Tofacitinib
interactions.description: L-Phenylalanine may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Tofacitinib.

interactions.name: Tacrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Tacrolimus is combined with L-Phenylalanine.

interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when L-Phenylalanine is combined with Clozapine.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with L-Phenylalanine.
